coloma2018.title: Argentine Spanish (Illustrations of the IPA)

cruzferreira1995.pagination: Printed folio in the running head; the article runs 90-94. The word lists are on printed page 91 (PDF page index 2). The PDF is a scan with an unreliable text layer — the rows were transcribed from a render of the page, not from pdftotext.

barbosa2004.title: Brazilian Portuguese (Illustrations of the IPA)
barbosa2004.venue: Journal of the International Phonetic Association 34(2):227-232
barbosa2004.pagination: Printed folio in the running head; the article runs 227-232. The consonant word list is on printed page 228 (PDF page index 2). The text layer mangles the IPA, so the rows were transcribed from a render of the page.

brissos2014.title: New insights into Portuguese central-southern dialects: understanding their present and past forms through acoustic data from stressed vowels
brissos2014.pagination: Printed folio in the running head (`66  Fernando Brissos` / `Portuguese central-southern dialects  67`).
brissos2014.describes: Portuguese central-southern dialects: CI = the central-interior subdialect (Castelo Branco / Beira Baixa); SW = the southwestern subdialect (Baixo Alentejo / Alentejo littoral)

laufer1999.title: Hebrew
laufer1999.venue: Handbook of the International Phonetic Association: A Guide to the Use of the International Phonetic Alphabet, Cambridge University Press, pp. 96-99 (Illustrations of the IPA; earlier version: Journal of the International Phonetic Association 20(2), 1990, 40-43)
laufer1999.pagination: Printed folio in the Handbook running head; the Hebrew chapter is printed pages 96-99 (the linked Internet Archive scan of the full Handbook was the copy consulted; the Cambridge record of the 1990 JIPA version is in the venue field). Page numbers cited are the printed Handbook pages.
laufer1999.describes: Modern Israeli Hebrew, Oriental and Non-Oriental pronunciations (two Israeli-born informants)

owens2006.title: A Linguistic History of Arabic

owens2006.describes: Nigerian Arabic (Shuwa), the West Sudanic / Lake Chad chain

prochazka2026.pagination: Open-access advance-article PDF; `page` records the printed folio shown in the article header (verified for pp.310, 318).

guerrero2019.title: Reflexes of Old Arabic */ǧ/ in the Maghrebi Dialects
guerrero2019.pagination: The published Arabica article runs pp.137-169; the open-access HAL preprint (hal-03516978v1) carries no printed folio, so `page` records the HAL preprint PDF page index (its two-page HAL cover/authorization sheet is pages 1-2, the article body begins on page 2).
guerrero2019.describes: Old Arabic *ǧ across the Maghreb (Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia, Libya, Mauritania/Ḥassāniyya)
